skill.name: github-workflow-automation
skill.description: Automate GitHub workflows with AI assistance. Includes PR reviews, issue triage, CI/CD integration, and Git operations.
skill.summary: GitHub Actions automation patterns with AI-powered PR reviews, issue triage, smart test selection, and deployment workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 136 patterns that are all false positives. The detected exec() calls are Node.js child_process functions for git commands, not Python exec(). Shell command substitutions and backticks are standard GitHub Actions workflow syntax. Environment variable access follows proper GitHub Secrets patterns. No malicious patterns, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. This is a legitimate DevOps documentation skill.

content.value_statement: Streamline your development workflow by integrating AI into GitHub Actions. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for automated PR reviews, intelligent issue triage, and CI/CD optimization.
actual_capabilities: Create automated PR review workflows using Anthropic API integration
Set up intelligent issue triage with automatic labeling and categorization
Implement smart test selection based on changed files
Configure deployment risk assessment with AI validation
Automate Git operations like rebasing and cherry-picking
Build on-demand AI assistance bots for PR comments and issues
limitations: Requires GitHub Actions environment and proper repository permissions
Anthropic API key must be configured as a GitHub Secret
Some workflows require GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed on runners
AI review latency depends on API response times and rate limits

use_cases.title: Engineering Team Lead
use_cases.description: Automate code review feedback for pull requests, ensuring consistent quality standards and catching common issues before human review.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Reduce CI/CD pipeline execution time by intelligently selecting which test suites to run based on the files changed in each pull request.

use_cases.title: Open Source Maintainer
use_cases.description: Automatically categorize and label incoming issues, request missing information from bug reporters, and identify first-time contributors.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR Review Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request diff and provide feedback on: 1) Summary of changes, 2) Potential bugs or issues, 3) Suggestions for improvement, 4) Security concerns. Format as GitHub-flavored markdown with specific line references where applicable.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting AI feedback on a pull request before human review

prompt_templates.title: Issue Triage Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this GitHub issue and return JSON with: type (bug/feature/question/docs/other), severity level, affected area, summary, whether repro steps are provided, and suggested labels. Issue title: {title}. Issue body: {body}
prompt_templates.scenario: Automatically categorizing new issues for faster routing

prompt_templates.title: Deployment Risk Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these deployment changes for risk. Return JSON with: riskLevel (low/medium/high), specific concerns, recommendations, and whether manual approval is required. Consider database migrations, breaking changes, and security implications. Changes: {changes}
prompt_templates.scenario: Pre-deployment validation before pushing to production

prompt_templates.title: Cherry-Pick Conflict Resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to cherry-pick commit {commitHash} to branch {targetBranch}. Analyze the commit diff and current state of affected files. Predict any merge conflicts and suggest a resolution strategy. Provide step-by-step commands to resolve conflicts if they occur.
prompt_templates.scenario: Applying hotfixes to multiple branches with minimal manual intervention

output_examples.input: PR review for a feature adding user authentication
output: Adds JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens.
- Line 42: Token expiration not validated before use
- Line 78: Password hashing uses default iterations - consider increasing
- Add rate limiting to login endpoint
- Implement token blacklist for logout functionality

output_examples.input: Issue: App crashes when uploading large files
output: Auto-applied labels: bug, priority: high, area: backend
Comment posted requesting: reproduction steps, file size details, server environment

best_practices: Store API keys and tokens in GitHub Secrets, never hardcode in workflow files
Use minimal required permissions in workflow job definitions (read/write scope as needed)
Implement timeouts for all jobs to prevent hung workflows and resource waste
Cache dependencies between runs to speed up workflow execution
Use path filters to skip irrelevant jobs when certain files are not changed
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API keys or tokens directly in workflow YAML files
Using overly permissive permissions like contents: write when only read is needed
Running AI review on every small change without filtering for significant diffs
Skipping status checks or bypassing branch protection for convenience

faq.question: What AI models does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Claude models (claude-3-sonnet-20240229 or newer). You can update the model parameter in workflow files to use other Claude variants like claude-3-opus or claude-3-haiku based on your needs.

faq.question: How do I configure the Anthropic API key?
faq.answer: Navigate to your repository Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions. Add a new secret named ANTHROPIC_API_KEY with your key value. The workflows reference this via ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}.

faq.question: Can this skill work with self-hosted runners?
faq.answer: Yes, all workflows are compatible with self-hosted runners. Update runs-on from ubuntu-latest to your self-hosted runner labels. This is useful for workflows requiring specific tools or faster execution.

faq.question: How do I customize the PR review prompts?
faq.answer: Modify the script content in the AI Review step of .github/workflows/ai-review.yml. You can add specific review criteria, focus areas, or integrate with your team's coding standards.

faq.question: What happens if the AI API rate limit is exceeded?
faq.answer: The workflow will fail. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff in your workflow scripts, or upgrade your Anthropic API plan for higher rate limits.

faq.question: Can I use this with private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, all workflows work with private repositories. The GITHUB_TOKEN automatically has access to the repository where the workflow runs. Ensure secrets are configured at the repository or organization level.
